acceptance_criteria: Artifact contains at least one full perturbation example with arithmetic steps.
Recalculated metrics are consistent with the computation script and actual_outputs.json derived_metrics format.
Reviewer can verify whether operational recommendation (5% tolerance) would change under the perturbations.
planned_changes.description: Add an explicit sensitivity-analysis section with at least three numeric perturbation examples: (1) rust row +2% label noise (move 2% of rust row off-diagonal proportionally), (2) rust row -2%, (3) combined +/-5% perturbation scenario. For each scenario show step-by-step recalculation of row/column totals, TP/FP/FN, per-class precision/recall, combined false-healthy counts/rates, and whether the 5% diseased-missed tolerance is exceeded.

acceptance_criteria: Code block matches the script in agent_trace.json or equivalent Python snippet.
Running the script reproduces the numbers in the artifact's derived_metrics section.
planned_changes.description: Insert the node script (from agent_trace.json) as a copy-pasteable code block in an 'Appendix: Reproducible calculations' section, with short usage instructions and expected output snippet.

acceptance_criteria: Decision table is present and cites the specific numeric triggers used in the computations.
Each action includes a recommended minimum sample size or monitoring recommendation.
planned_changes.description: Add a concise final decision table that maps numeric triggers (e.g., combined false-healthy > 5%, rust false-healthy > 10%) to concrete remediation steps (e.g., 'require human review for N samples per field', 'deploy secondary diagnostic test', 'hold autonomous healthy clearance') and recommended validation sample sizes for each action.

acceptance_criteria: Artifact preview is not truncated in the verification environment (or the full file is available as an attachment).
Verifier can read the sensitivity section and decision table without truncation.
planned_changes.description: Ensure the full Markdown artifact is attached/available to graders/verifiers (avoid preview truncation): either extend the previewable portion or attach the full file in the delivery channel. Document in artifact header that the artifact includes sensitivity section and decision table.

acceptance_criteria: No duplicated/conflicting score values remain in grader/verifier outputs.
Rubric item ri_5 appears only in failed_rubric_items until sensitivity is added and validated.
Artifact footer documents which score field is authoritative.
planned_changes.description: Update the attempt metadata and any grader-facing fields so that score fields and pass/fail lists are consistent: ensure 'final_score' equals the computed weighted score and that rubric items appear only in either passed_criteria or failed_criteria. Add a short 'metadata reconciliation' note in artifact footer explaining the authoritative score field.

acceptance_criteria: Percentages use consistent decimal formatting across the artifact.
A one-line updated interpretation of risk is present in the executive summary.
planned_changes.description: Increase numeric reporting precision where useful (report percentages with 3-4 decimal places) and add a one-line summary of the updated risk posture after sensitivity analysis.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The audit artifact presents correct and reproducible confusion-matrix arithmetic, clear false-healthy quantification, and actionable operational recommendations tied to numeric thresholds. However, the sensitivity-analysis requirement (rubric item ri_5) is not verifiably present in the visible artifact preview (preview truncated) and the grader/verifier outputs show internal metadata inconsistencies (duplicate/conflicting score fields and rubric pass/fail lists). To reach a fully passing revision: (1) add explicit numeric sensitivity perturbation examples with arithmetic and resulting metric recalculations; (2) include a concise final decision table mapping numeric triggers to remediation steps and validation sample sizes; (3) embed the computation script/pseudocode as a code block for copy-paste reproducibility; and (4) resolve/report metadata inconsistencies so the grader/verifier outputs consistently reflect the same pass/fail and score values.
